Sunday, September 29, 2024
Armenia
Welcome to
 Arrival and Check-in to the Hotel
 Arrival and Check-in to the Hotel
 3:00 P.M. ~ 4:00 P.M. - Yerevan City Tour
 10:00 A.M. ~ 6:00 P.M. - Registration
 The registration desk will be located in the hotel lobby
 5:00 P.M. ~ 6:30 P.M. - Yerevan Brandy Company
 
The DoubleTree by Hilton 
 4/2 Grigor Lusavorich Street, 
Yerevan 0015, Armenia
For those arriving early, the Ministry of Economy of Armenia has planned a captivating tour of some of Yerevan’s key landmarks, 
starting at 3:00 p.m. and lasting approximately one hour. This is a perfect opportunity to explore Yerevan’s heritage before the 
program begins. We recommend wearing comfortable shoes for this walking tour.
 
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
 
This reception provides delegates with the perfect opportunity to connect and get to know each other before the busy 
week kicks off on Monday morning. Exclusively for ACE Armenia participants, the event will offer a relaxed setting to build 
relationships and familiarize themselves with their fellow attendees.
Yerevan Brandy Company will host the distinguished delegates of ACE Armenia, providing attendees with the opportunity 
to experience the renowned warmth of Armenian hospitality. 
The history of brandy-making in Armenia dates back to 1887 when the Yerevan brandy 
factory was built and put into operation. Since then, Armenian brandy production has grown 
significantly. As of today, there are approximately 30 brandy-producing organizations in 
Armenia, with an annual production volume exceeding 20 million liters. Armenian brandy 
is exported to around 50 countries worldwide. Armenian brandy has won 170 medals in 
international competitions, including prestigious gold medals in Paris, Liège, and Bordeaux. 
The Yerevan brandy company is one of the largest organizations in Armenia, playing a vital 
role in the country’s economic development.
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
 
 6:30 p.m. 
 
 transfer to the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan 
 7:00 P.M. ~ 9:00 P.M. - Meet and Greet Reception
Attire: Business Casual

Monday, September 30, 2024
Shaping the Future: 
Education, Innovation, and Sustainability
Day 1
 7:50 a.m. - Meet in Lobby / load buses 
 Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan 
 8:00 A.M.~ 8:20 A.M. 
 
 transfer to American University of Armenia
 8:30 A.M. ~ 1:00 P.M. - American University of Armenia 
The American University of Armenia (AUA) is a distinguished 
private institution of higher education in Armenia, offering a 
world-class academic experience that combines international 
standards with a local context. Affiliated with the University 
of California and accredited by the WASC Senior College and 
University Commission, AUA is committed to providing a high-
quality, graduate, and undergraduate education. AUA is currently 
the only university in the former Soviet Union that received WASC 
Senior College and University Commission accreditation. 
• Official opening ceremony - 8:45 A.M. 
(Alex and Marie Manoogian Hall)
• Welcome Remarks: 8:45 a.m.
Dr. Alina Gharabegian, Provost, American University of 
Armenia
• ACE Armenia Opening Remarks: 9:00 a.m.
Gevorg Papoyan, Minister of Economy, Republic of Armenia
Narek Hovakimyan, Deputy Minister of Economy, Republic 
of Armenia
Kristina Kvien, U.S. Ambassador to the Republic of Armenia
Francisco O. Mora, Ambassador, Permanent Representative, 
Permanent Mission to the OAS, U.S. Department of State 
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
 Coffee Break - 9:40 A.M 
• Panel discussion - 10:30 A.M 
Harnessing Climate Challenges: Transforming Risks into 
Opportunities for Climate-Resilient Investments and a 
Sustainable Future
Panel Moderator: Alen Gasparian Amirkhanian, Director, AUA
Acopian Center for the Environment 
Panelists: Artur Alaverdyan, Co-founder, SolarOn / 
Erik Grigoryan, f. Minister of Environment, Founder & CEO, 
Environment Group / Khachatur Meliksetian, Director, Institute 
of Geological Sciences, Armenian National Academy of 
Sciences / Astghine Pasoyan, Executive Director, Energy Saving 
Foundation
• AUA tour: Large Auditorium, EPIC, Media Lab - 11:50 A.M. 
Entrepreneurship and Product Innovation Center (epic) - is AUA’s on-
campus start-up incubator that helps University students, 
alumni, and other entrepreneurs advance their ventures from 
idea to success. It provides an ecosystem that consists of first-
class facilities and collaboration spaces, educational programs 
and events, and a network of mentors, advisors, and investors.
Media Lab - Was established with the support of the USAID 
to promote civil discourse in Armenia. The Lab aims to 
strengthen democratic culture, promote the development of 
a civic press, foster citizen engagement in social media, and 
advocate for the proper discernment of information. 
 1:15 P.M. ~ 1:30 P.M. 
 
 transfer to Matena
 1:30 P.M. ~ 4:00 P.M. - Matena 
Matena is an Armenia-based international school that contributes 
to the development of professional skills and leadership qualities 
through collecting and sharing the best knowledge and the 
leading world practices for those who permanently pursue 
improvement and excellence in their professional fields.
 Networking Lunch - 1:40 P.M.
• MATENA Presentation - 2:30 P.M.
Ruben Hayrapetyan, Co-founder, CEO, Matena
• UWC Presentation - 2:40 P.M.
Adam Armanski, Head of College and Director, DISA Foundation

Shaping Tomorrow’s Workforce: Redefining Talent Development

Panel Moderator: Maria Titizian, Editor-In-Chief, EVN Report

Director, Global Head of Learning & Development, Picsart /

 4:15 P.M. ~ 4:30 P.M .
 
 transfer to TUMO
4:30 P.M. ~ 6:00 P.M. - TUMO Yerevan 
• Tour & Presentation at TUMO Yerevan
Our visit to the TUMO Center for Creative Technologies offers a 
unique opportunity to explore a renowned Armenian brand that 
has made its mark internationally. The TUMO Center for Creative 
Technologies is a free-of-charge educational program that puts 
teens in charge of their own learning. Tumo’s learning program 
is made up of self-learning activities, workshops and project labs 
that revolve around 14 learning targets.
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
6:20 P.M. ~ 7:10 P.M. - Picsart Visit 
Picsart is an Armenian-American technology company with its 
headquarters located in Miami, Florida and Yerevan, Armenia. 
The company specializes in the development of the Picsart 
suite, a collection of online photo and video editing applications 
combined with a social creative community. In 2022, Picsart 
achieved unicorn status after raising $130 million in funding 
from SoftBank.

 Transfer to Restaurant
 Park Bridge Restaurant

Tourism Committee Presentation
 9:40 P.M. ~ 10:00 P.M. - 
 Transfer to 
the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan 
Breakfast starting from 7:00 a.m. at the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan Attire: Business

TUESDAY, October 1, 2024
Day 2
Breakfast starting from 6:30 a.m. at the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan Attire: Business/business casual
 7:15 a.m. - Meet in Lobby / load buses 
 Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan 
 7:25 A.M. ~ 7:50 A.M. 
 
 transfer to Engineering City
 8:00 A.M. ~ 9:30 A.M. - ENGINEERING CITY TOUR AND 
PRESENTATION
Participants will have the opportunity to visit Engineering 
City, a dedicated environment for high-tech engineering 
companies. This visit aims to provide insights into the 
facilities, resources, and collaborative opportunities offered 
by Engineering City for companies in the field of engineering, 
science, and education. 
• Engineering City tour - 8:00 A.M. 
Engineering city - an environment designed for high-
tech engineering companies, aimed at facilitating and 
expediting the development of intricate engineering 
solutions. It serves as a collaborative platform where 
engineering, science, and education converge to address 
the advanced engineering challenges of the 21st century. 
• ENGINEERING CITY PRESENTATION - 8:50 A.M. 
Join us for an exclusive session led by Aram Salatian, 
the visionary Director of Engineering City, as he offers a 
comprehensive overview of the remarkable progress being 
achieved in onsite manufacturing. This session will also 
delve into the ambitious expansion plans on the horizon 
for Engineering City. Aram Salatian will share insights 
into upcoming infrastructure developments, strategic 
partnerships, and initiatives aimed at scaling operations to 
meet the growing demands of the industry.
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
 9:30 A.M. ~ 9:50 A.M. 
 
 transfer to Cascade Royal
 10:00 A.M. ~ 12:00 P.M. - Cascade Royal 
 NETWORKING Brunch - 10:00 A.M.
This networking space will allow to continue discussions 
on potential collaborations with Armenian businesses and 
business associations.
 12:00 P.M. ~ 12:15 P.M. 
 
 transfer to ServiceTitan
12:15 P.M. ~ 1:15 P.M. - ServiceTitan 
ServiceTitan is an Armenian-founded tech unicorn. CEO Ara 
Mahdessian and President Vahe Kuzoyan, both of Armenian 
descent, joined forces after college and founded ServiceTitan 
with a simple but challenging goal: to streamline their fathers’ 
home service businesses. Now, their company is responsible for 
transforming the entire home services industry. ServiceTitan is the 
world’s leading and fastest-growing software technology platform 
for the trades, a trillion-dollar global industry. 
• Service Titan Tour and Presentation - 12:15 P.M.
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
 1:15 P.M. ~ 2:00 P.M. 
 
 Transfer to Woods Center
2:00 P.M. ~ 9:00 P.M .- Woods Center 
Woods Center, is a multi-purpose hub that offers a unique 
environment for business, education, events, and living. More 
than just a business center, Woods is a thoughtfully designed 
space where individuals can work, learn, and entertain, all 
within an inspiring and vibrant atmosphere. Delegates will 
explore how this innovative space fosters collaboration and 
creativity across various fields.
 LUNCH - 2:00 P.M. 
• Woods Center & Ardy Academy Tour - 2:40 P.M. 
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
Ardy Academy - Founded by Renderforest, Ardy Academy is a 
groundbreaking educational initiative focused on training 
individuals and teams for key digital professions that are 
integral to the fourth industrial revolution in the Republic 
of Armenia. Through an intensive training program, the 
academy aims to equip students with the skills they need 
for the digital age. Known as “Ardy Academy”, this institution 
stands as the largest center for digital professions in Armenia.
• Woods Center & Renderforest Presentation - 3:50 P.M. 
Renderforest is an all-in-one branding platform offering users the 
best online tools to create high-quality videos, graphic designs, 
logos, mockups, and websites with minimal time and effort. 
Nare Gasparyan, CTO, Director of Engineering, Renderforest
• Presentation by Union of Advanced Technology 
Enterprises UATE - 4:05 P.M. 
Edita Ghazaryan - Deputy CEO, UATE
• Panel discussion - 4:20 P.M. 
Fostering Innovation: Building Ecosystems that Support

Panel Moderator: Raffi Kassarjian, CEO and Principal 
Advisor, Sensyan
Panelists: Hovsep Patvakanyan, Head at Investment

/ Sona Veziryan, Director, BANA Angels / Arik Akverdian,

• Sunset Reception & Networking with

• Startup Pitch Session - 7:00 P.M. 
The day will finish with a startup-pitch session. In collaboration 
with the Innovation Exchange Coalition (IEC) and the STAN 
Science and Technology Angel Network, our preselected 
startups will have the exciting opportunity to pitch their 
innovative ideas. This session offers not only the chance 
to present in front of the delegates but also to pitch to 
angel investors from Armenia and online from Los Angeles.

 Transfer to 
the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan
Driving Innovation: 
Technology, Startups, and Entrepreneurship

Wednesday, October 2, 2024
Breakfast starting from 6:30 a.m. at the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan

 Transfer to Yeremyan Tashir Farm
10:35 A.M. ~ 11:40 A.M. - Yeremyan Tashir Farm 
• Yeremyan Tashir Farm Tour 
Yeremyan Tashir Farm, a state-of-the-art livestock complex 
spanning over 30 hectares, plays a critical role in advancing 
Armenia’s agriculture and food security, with a focus on 
sustainable agriculture and economic development, the visit 
will provide valuable insights into how innovative farming 
practices are contributing to the nation’s food production 
and rural growth.
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
 11:40 A.M. ~ 1:20 P.M.
 
 Transfer to COAF
1:20 P.M. ~ 4:30 P.M. - Children of Armenia Fund - COAF 
 Lunch - 1:30 P.M.
• COAF Tour - 2:20 P.M.
Our visit to the Children of Armenia Fund (COAF) is an 
opportunity to witness the transformative work of a non-
profit organization dedicated to enhancing educational 
opportunities and infrastructure in rural areas of Armenia. 
ACE leaders will explore potential avenues for collaboration 
and the exportation of this impactful initiative model. COAF’s 
work stands as a testament to the power of education and 
sustainable development in rural communities, aligning with 
our shared commitment to positive change and progress.
• Presentations - 3:10 P.M.
Gurgen Hakobyan, Operations Advisor, COAF
• Panel discussion - 3:20 P.M. 
Innovating Agriculture: Driving Sustainable Farming 
Practices for Enhanced Food Security
Panel Moderator: Artak Ghazaryan, Chief of Party of 
USAID/Economic Foundations for a Resilient Armenia 
Activity
Panelists: Arman Khojoyan, Deputy Minister of Economy
/ Emin Martirosyan, Managing Partner, SkyAgro 
/ Liana Ghaltaghchyan, Executive Director, COAF 
/ David Yeremyan, Founder & General Director, Yeremyan 
Projects
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
 
 4:30 P.M. ~ 7:20 P.M.
 
 Transfer to Yerevan
7:30 P.M. ~ 9:30 P.M. - Yerevan - dinner hosted by the 
Minister of Economy
After yet another long day our dinner at Megerian Carpet 
will be a journey through history and artistry. The Megerian 
Family, pioneers in the rug trade, established their business 
in the United States in 1917. For generations, they have been 
deeply involved in the sale, restoration, and production of 
exquisite oriental rugs.
In addition to dinner, attendees will also be treated to a 
special master class and tour, providing a firsthand look 
at the craftsmanship behind these remarkable rugs. This 
experience will allow participants to explore the traditional 
techniques and cultural heritage that make Megerian Carpet 
a renowned name in the world of fine carpets.
 9:30 P.M. ~ 9:45 P.M. - 
 Transfer to 
the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan 
Day 3
Growing Smarter: 
Innovation and Sustainability in 
Agriculture and Farming

Thursday, October 3, 2024
Breakfast starting from 6:30 a.m. at the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan Attire: Business/business casual
 7:15 a.m. - Meet in Lobby / load buses - 
Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan 
 7:30 A.M. ~ 9:30 A.M. 
 
 Transfer to Hin Areni Factory
9:30 A.M. ~ 10:30 a.M. - Hin Areni Factory
• Hin Areni Factory Tour - 9:30 A.M.
Vayots Dzor and Areni village in particular has been the 
cradle of Armenian winemaking for millennia. “Hin Areni” 
wine factory opens up the doors of this region to the world 
to showcase the historic and indigenous Armenian grape 
varietals. At the winery the historic traditions of winemaking 
are well combined with state-of-the-art modern equipment. 
“Hin Areni” wine factory has the capacity to process over 
250 tonnes of grapes.
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
• Visit to Areni-1 Cave - 10:45 A.M.
What initially started as a modest project in 2007 evolved 
into one of the most remarkable archaeological discoveries
in the realm of historic winemaking and the traditions of 
Neolithic civilizations. Nestled near the historic Areni village
and the Arpa River, the Areni-1 Cave holds not only artifacts
but also grape stems and seeds discovered at the base of 
amphorae, known as karas. These artifacts were carefully 
examined, and the results unveiled a stunning revelation: 
the grape seeds date back an astonishing 6,200 years! This
groundbreaking discovery confirms that the Areni-1 Cave is
home to the earliest known winery in the world.
• Visit to Noravank Monastery - 11:45 A.M.
Noravank means “New Monastery” in Armenian. However, 
today this name does not fit, as the monastery is already 
more than seven centuries old. The Noravank Monastery 
was built in the 13th century on the ledge of narrow 
winding gorge above the Amaghu River, near the city of 
Yeghegnadzor. The spare lines of the church contrast with 
the sharp red rock cliffs, and the dramatic scenery is one of 
the best reasons to visit Noravank.
 12:45 P.M. ~ 1:00 P.M.
 
 Transfer to Noa Winery
1:00 P.M. ~ 4:40 P.M. - Noa Winery
 
 Networking Lunch - 1:00 P.M.

• My Armenia Program Presentation - 2:40 P.M.
Ashkhen Khudaverdyan, Project Manager at 
Smithsonian Institution
 
• Panel Discussion - 3:00 P.M.
Wine in a Changing World: Innovating Viticulture and 
Winemaking to Combat Climate Challenges and Revitalize 
Global Demand 
Panel Moderator: Mariam Saghatelyan, Co-Owner, 
InVIno
Panelists: Zaruhi Muradyan, Executive Director, Vine and 
Wine Foundation / Artak Gabrielyan, Chief winemaker-
viticulturist, Tavitian Vineyards / Areg Khojoyan, 
Director, GN Winery / Aimee Keushguerian, Founder, 
Owner, Zulal Wines
ACE leaders will discover the journey and future of viticulture 
in 
Armenia. 
The 
program 
includes 
knowledgeable 
representatives from both the private sector, including wine 
makers, and the Vine and Wine Foundation. This session 
will bring valuable insights into Armenia’s rich winemaking 
heritage, its current achievements, and the prospects 
that lie ahead. This is an excellent opportunity to explore 
potential avenues for collaboration and partnerships, where 
Armenia’s unique wine industry can intersect with the 
Americas.
 
• Networking session - 4:00 P.M.
 5:00 P.M. ~ 7:00 P.M.
 
 Transfer to Yerevan
7:00 P.M. ~ 9:00 P.M. - Networking Reception
 9:00 P.M. ~ 9:20 P.M. -

the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan 
Day 4
From Heritage to Innovation: 
The Evolution of Viticulture and Winemaking

FriDAY, October 4, 2024
Breakfast starting from 6:30 a.m. at the Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan
Attire: Business/business casual for the day and formal/semi formal for the gala dinner & closing ceremony
 7:15 a.m. - Meet in Lobby / load buses 
 Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton, Yerevan 
 7:30 A.M. ~ 9:30 A.M. 
 
 Transfer to Gyumri
9:30 A.M. ~ 10:45 A.M. - Gyumri Technology Center
The Gyumri Technology Center (GTC) has been established 
by the Enterprise Incubator Foundation, the RA Government 
and the World Bank. The goals of the Center include 
development of technical and business skills, promotion 
of 
technological 
entrepreneurship, 
commercialization 
of innovative research undertakings, creation of new 
technology companies, attracting of foreign investment and 
others.
• Presentation - 9:30 A.M.
Bagrat Yengibaryan, Director, Enterprise Incubator 
Foundation (EIF)
• Gyumri Information Technologies Center Presentation 
on programs focused on women economic empowerment 
- 9:40 A.M.
Amalya Yeghoyan, Executive Director, GITC
• Tour - 10:20 A.M.
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
 10:45 A.M. ~ 11:50 A.M.
 • Walking from GTC to Aregak Bakery - 10:45 A.M.
 
 
 Coffee Break at Aregak Bakery - 11:00 A.M.
 Aregak Bakery is a remarkable establishment with a 
special mission. Aregak stands as the first inclusive 
bakery and café in Gyumri and in Armenia, where 
they take pride in employing both youth with 
disabilities and mothers of youth with disabilities. 
This visit not only offers a delightful break but also 
an opportunity to appreciate and contribute to 
the 
inclusive 
employment 
initiatives 
in 
Gyumri. 
 12:00 P.M. ~ 2:00 P.M. 
 
 Transfer to Yerevan
2:00 P.M. ~ 3:30 P.M. - AI9 Visit
AI9 is an international startup campus located in Yerevan, 
Armenia, set to open by the end of 2024. With a strong focus 
on artificial intelligence, innovation, and fostering a vibrant 
community of visionary tech founders, AI9 is designed to be 
a dynamic hub for startups. The campus aims to strengthen 
the local tech ecosystem while providing the tools, 
resources, and global connections needed to help startups 
grow and scale internationally. By nurturing collaboration 
and innovation, AI9 will play a pivotal role in shaping the 
future of Armenia’s technology landscape.
 3:35 P.M. ~ 3:50 P.M. 
 
 Transfer to SolarOn
3:50 P.M. ~ 4:30 P.M. - SolarOn
• SolarOn Presentation - 3:50 P.M.
Our visit to SolarOn Manufacturing Facility offers a 
firsthand look at Armenia’s commitment to sustainable 
and renewable energy solutions. Armenia is making strides 
towards a more eco-friendly and sustainable energy future. 
During this visit, participants will learn more about the 
solar panel production facilities, gaining insights into the 
processes and technologies driving Armenia’s renewable 
energy initiatives. This experience provides a valuable 
opportunity for a discussion on renewable energy and 
sustainability. We will also delve into potential opportunities 
for public-private collaboration, allowing participants to 
contribute to Armenia’s journey towards a more sustainable 
energy landscape. 
• ACE Armenia Group Photo 
 4:30 P.M. ~ 4:45 P.M.
 
 Transfer to HOtel
4:45 P.M. ~ 5:45 P.M.- Hotel DoubleTree by Hilton 
• Free time before the Closing Ceremony
 5:45 P.M. ~ 6:00 P.M.
 
 Transfer to Livingstone Restaurant
6:00P.M. ~ 9:00 P.M. - Livingstone Restaurant 
• Dinner & Closing Ceremony - 6:00 P.M.
Our final agenda item is a moment of celebration and 
reflection as we come together for dinner and the 
closing ceremony. This evening is a culmination of the 
experiences, insights, and connections forged during ACE 
19. We will reflect on the journey we’ve undertaken and 
the new friendships and collaborations that have emerged 
throughout our time together. This evening is a toast to new 
beginnings and the continuation of the relationships and 
initiatives that have been sparked during ACE Armenia.
• Official Program Closing Remarks - 7:00 P.M.
Gevorg Papoyan, Minister of Economy, Republic of 
Armenia
Francisco O. Mora, Ambassador, Permanent

Department of State 
La Celia Prince, Ambassador, Chief of Staff of the

 AND HOST SITES - 7:45 P.M.
